You always believed in me even when I didn’t believe in myself. Thank you for being there every step of the way. Without your guidance and motivational speeches, I wouldn’t be here today.

And now, after months of preparations, I can finally say that I did it – I ran a marathon! It feels amazing knowing that all of my efforts paid off.

This experience taught me not only how strong I could be physically, but also mentally. With determination and resilience, anything is possible. I will never forget what I accomplished today and I hope to continue pushing boundaries as I move forward.

Thank you again for believing in me and helping me reach this goal.

instruction: What are the steps to using random (yet coherent) text as filler content within your Word document?
output:
1. Open Microsoft Word and create a new blank document or open an existing one. 
2. Select "Insert" from the menu bar, then select "Text". This will bring up a box where you can type in whatever words/text you want for the filler content of your document. You may also use the "Paste" option if you already have some pre-written text saved on your computer.
3. Once you've added the desired amount of text into the box, click “OK” to insert it onto the page. If necessary, adjust the font size, color, and style by clicking on the appropriate buttons at the top of the window.
4. To make sure there aren’t any unwanted spaces between paragraphs, press Ctrl + Enter after typing each line of text. Doing this will ensure all lines automatically connect without any extra spacing.
5. Finally, once everything looks good, save the file with a name and location of your choice. Your document should now contain the desired filler content!

Period dramas often feature elaborate sets, lavish costumes, and intricate storylines to capture the essence of another time period. They typically focus on themes such as love, loss, family dynamics, social class struggles, political upheaval, and other timeless human experiences. 

Examples of period dramas include Downton Abbey (2010-2015), Poldark (2015–present), The Crown (2016–present), Outlander (2014–present), Game Of Thrones (2011–present) and Victoria (2017–present). These series have captivated audiences with their sweeping stories, stunning visuals, and compelling characters. 

The popularity of period dramas has grown exponentially over recent years due to advances in technology which allow for more realistic depictions of bygone eras. Additionally, these types of films provide viewers with a unique opportunity to explore different cultures and lifestyles from centuries ago. Furthermore, they can be used to educate people about important events in history while entertaining at the same time.

instruction:
Write a simple guide for uploading the bibliography database
 on Overleaf.
output:
1. Open your browser and navigate to https://www.overleaf.com/ 
2. Click "Sign in" at the top right corner of the page, enter your username or email address and password, then click Sign In. 
3. On the left sidebar, select “Biblio” from the list of options. This will open up the Biblio section where you can create new databases. 
4. Select “Create New Database”. A blank form with fields labeled Title, Author(s), Publication Information, etc., will appear. Fill out all relevant information about your bibliographic data. You may also choose to add additional columns such as ISBN numbers, DOIs, etc. 
5. Once finished, press the blue “Save & Publish” button located at the bottom of the form. Your newly created bibliographic database should now be visible under the “My Databases” tab. 
6. To start adding records to your bibliographic database, simply click on any field within the table and begin typing. When done entering records, press the green “Publish” button at the bottom of the screen to save them.

4. “Scalability Limits of Supervised Learning Algorithms” by Andrew Ng et al., Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 15 (NIPS’03), pp. 1429–1436 (2003).

Blog title: Healthy Apple Muffins
Blog Topic: recipe and tips for cooking a good apple muffin
output:
Are you looking for a healthier alternative to your regular breakfast treat? Look no further than these delicious, nutritious Apple Muffins! Made with fresh apples, oats, almond flour, coconut oil, honey and spices, they are sure to satisfy any sweet craving without all of the guilt. 
In addition to being packed full of flavorful ingredients, these muffins also contain plenty of fiber from the oatmeal and apples as well as healthy fats from the coconut oil and honey. They are also low in sugar and easy to customize by adding nuts or dried fruit. So what are you waiting for? Get ready to make some tasty treats that will give you energy throughout the day! 
Let’s get started making these amazingly moist and delicious Apple Muffins! Firstly, preheat your oven to 350°F (176°C). In a large bowl mix together 2 cups of old-fashioned rolled oats, ½ cup of chopped apples, ¼ teaspoon each of cinnamon, ginger and cloves, ⅛ teaspoon of salt and ¾ cup of shredded coconut. Then add 4 tablespoons of melted coconut oil, 1 egg and 1 tablespoon of honey and whisk until everything is combined. Finally, fold in 1 cup of almond flour until just mixed. 
Grease a muffin tin and divide the batter evenly among them. Bake for about 20 minutes or until golden brown. Let cool before serving. Enjoy!

adversity
output:
Adversity and hardship are both words used to describe difficult or unpleasant situations or circumstances. Adversity can refer to any kind of difficulty or misfortune while hardship implies more extreme difficulties than adversity. 

Adversity often involves some form of struggle or challenge which must be overcome before progressing further whereas hardship usually refers to a situation where there is no way out but to endure it until better times come. For example, someone who has lost their job may face adversity as they look for another one, however if they have been laid off with no chance of returning then this would constitute hardship. 

In terms of usage, adversity is generally used when referring to general struggles or challenges whilst hardship is typically reserved for particularly trying conditions such as poverty, illness, imprisonment, warfare, etc. Additionally, adversity tends to imply a longer duration of time compared to hardship which suggests something more immediate and intense. 

Overall, adversity and hardship are two different concepts which should not be confused. Adversity is a broader term which encompasses all kinds of difficult experiences, whereas hardship specifically relates to especially arduous conditions.

instruction:
Discuss the origins and history of the word that has been given to you.

oblique
output: The word 'oblique' comes from the Latin obliquus, meaning "slanting" or "sloping". It was first used in English around 1400 as an adjective to describe something slanted at an angle. The noun form came into use later in the same century with the meaning “an inclination to one side; a sloping position.” In its earliest usage, it referred specifically to angles on a map which were not perpendicular but instead angled away from each other. This sense of ‘oblique’ remained popular until the mid-20th century when it began to be used more generally for any kind of slant or tilt. Today, we most commonly use this term to refer to an attitude or opinion which differs from what is considered mainstream or accepted. For example, someone might say they have an ‘oblique viewpoint’ on a particular issue. Additionally, ‘obliques’ are also known as diagonals in mathematics and geometry. They represent lines drawn between two points which are not necessarily opposite each other like normal straight lines do.

instruction: Write a step-by-step guide for resolving GitHub merge conflicts.
output:
Step 1: Open your repository and locate the branch you want to merge into the master branch. In this example, we will be merging Branch A into Master.

Step 2: Run `git checkout master` in order to switch branches so that you are on the master branch. This will also show any uncommitted changes which need to be resolved before continuing with the merge.

Step 3: Run `git merge BranchA` to begin the merge process. This command will start comparing the two branches and create a list of differences between them. It may take some time depending on how many files have been changed or added since the last commit.

Step 4: Once the merge has finished, you should see a conflict message appear at the bottom of the screen. The exact text displayed depends on what type of change was made (deletion vs addition) but it typically looks like "Your branch is ahead of 'master' by XX commits". Click on the "Merge" button next to the conflict marker to open up the Merge Conflict window.

Step 5: On the left hand side of the Merge Conflict Window, select the file(s) that contain the conflicting changes from both branches. You can do this by clicking on each individual file name listed underneath “Unmerged paths”.

Step 6: On the right hand side of the Merge Conflict Window, review the changes that were made in each file and decide which set of changes you would prefer to keep. To resolve the conflict, click on either the “ours” or “theirs” icon to indicate whether you wish to accept their changes or yours respectively. If there are multiple lines of code that need to be changed, use the three dots menu option to choose which line of code you would like to edit first.

Step 7: After making all desired edits, click on the “Apply” button located at the top of the Merge Conflict Window to save your changes. Your local repository should now reflect the changes made during the merge.

Step 8: Finally, run `git push origin master` to update your remote repository with the new changes.

instruction:
Answer the following question.

How do I compare strings in Java?
I've been using the == operator in my program to compare all my strings so far. However, I ran into a bug, changed one of them into .equals() instead, and it fixed the bug.
Is == bad? When should it and should it not be used? What's the difference?
output:
The == (equal sign) operator compares two objects and returns true if they are equal or false if they are different. This means that two Strings with exactly the same characters will return true when compared with this operator. 

However, there can be cases where you want to check for equality but the contents may differ slightly - such as comparing two versions of a file name. In these situations, you would use the .equals(String s) method which checks whether the object references are identical and also checks the content by comparing each character within the string. If both methods return true then the strings are considered equivalent; otherwise, they are not.

Using == incorrectly can lead to unexpected results since it only tests for reference equality rather than actual content equality. For example, if you have two String variables called "name1" and "name2", and you assign the value "John Doe" to both names, then their values will be the same even though the content differs. Using .equals(), however, will detect that the strings are different because the content has changed.

instruction:
You are given two questions. Check if they are duplicates.

Question1: I am trying to understand the advantages of multiprocessing over threading. I know that multiprocessing gets around the Global Interpreter Lock, but what other advantages are there, and can threading not do the same thing?
Question2: I am learning how to use the threading and the multiprocessing modules in Python to run certain operations in parallel and speed up my code.
I am finding this hard (maybe because I don't have any theoretical background about it) to understand what the difference is between a threading.Thread() object and a multiprocessing.Process() one.
Also, it is not entirely clear to me how to instantiate a queue of jobs and having only 4 (for example) of them running in parallel, while the other wait for resources to free before being executed.
I find the examples in the documentation clear, but not very exhaustive; as soon as I try to complicate things a bit, I receive a lot of weird errors (like a method that can't be pickled, and so on).
So, when should I use the threading and multiprocessing modules?
Can you link me to some resources that explain the concepts behind these two modules and how to use them properly for complex tasks?
output:
Duplication Detected! Questions 1 & 2 appear to be asking the same question with different wordings. We will mark both questions as duplicated and provide the following answer which addresses both queries.

The main advantage of using multiprocessing instead of threads is that processes share memory space, allowing multiple computations or data manipulations to occur simultaneously within the same program. Threads, however, must create their own separate memory spaces, meaning each thread operates independently from the others and cannot take advantage of shared memory. Additionally, multiprocessing allows for more efficient resource utilization since multiple processes can access the same resources at once without needing to synchronize access. Finally, multiprocessing also offers greater scalability than threading due to its ability to handle larger numbers of concurrently executing processes.

Zinc is an essential mineral that is required for good health. It is involved in many biochemical processes in the body and is necessary for the proper functioning of the immune system. Excess zink is not harmful as it is excreted in the urine.
output:
Zinc is an essential mineral that is required for good health. It is involved in many biochemical processes in the body, including wound healing, cell division, and the production of proteins and hormones. Zinc also plays a role in maintaining a strong immune system by helping to fight off bacterial and viral infections. 
Excess zinc intake can lead to adverse effects such as nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and stomach cramps. However, these symptoms are usually mild and go away on their own within a few days. In rare cases, high levels of zinc may interfere with copper absorption, which could cause problems with the nervous system or kidneys. Long-term excessive consumption of zinc supplements has been linked to low blood sugar levels and other complications. Therefore, it’s important to consult your doctor before taking large doses of zinc supplements. 
Overall, zinc is an important nutrient that helps keep our bodies running smoothly. To get enough zinc in your daily diet, include food sources like oysters, pumpkin seeds, lamb, crab, cashew nuts, spinach, chickpeas, yogurt, and fortified breakfast cereals.

instruction:
Summarize the article you have been given in a brief manner.

Mathematics and art are related in a variety of ways. Mathematics has itself been described as an art motivated by beauty. Mathematics can be discerned in arts such as music, dance, painting, architecture, sculpture, and textiles. This article focuses, however, on mathematics in the visual arts.
Mathematics and art have a long historical relationship. Artists have used mathematics since the 4th century BC when the Greek sculptor Polykleitos wrote his Canon, prescribing proportions conjectured to have been based on the ratio 1:√2 for the ideal male nude. Persistent popular claims have been made for the use of the golden ratio in ancient art and architecture, without reliable evidence. In the Italian Renaissance, Luca Pacioli wrote the influential treatise De divina proportione (1509), illustrated with woodcuts by Leonardo da Vinci, on the use of the golden ratio in art. Another Italian painter, Piero della Francesca, developed Euclid's ideas on perspective in treatises such as De Prospectiva Pingendi, and in his paintings. The engraver Albrecht Dürer made many references to mathematics in his work Melencolia I. In modern times, the graphic artist M. C. Escher made intensive use of tessellation and hyperbolic geometry, with the help of the mathematician H. S. M. Coxeter, while the De Stijl movement led by Theo van Doesburg and Piet Mondrian explicitly embraced geometrical forms. Mathematics has inspired textile arts such as quilting, knitting, cross-stitch, crochet, embroidery, weaving, Turkish and other carpet-making, as well as kilim. In Islamic art, symmetries are evident in forms as varied as Persian girih and Moroccan zellige tilework, Mughal jali pierced stone screens, and widespread muqarnas vaulting.
output: The history between mathematics and art dates back centuries, with artists using mathematical principles to create works of art. For example, the Golden Ratio was believed to be used extensively in Ancient Greek art and architecture, although there is no conclusive proof. During the Renaissance period, Luca Pacioli’s book “De Divina Proportione” further explored this concept, which was then taken up again during the Modernist era by painters like Piero della Francesca and M.C. Escher. More recently, geometric patterns and symmetry have become increasingly prevalent in contemporary art, particularly within textile artforms such as quilt making, knitting, and weaving.

output: The “fair trade” movement, also known as the “trade justice” movement, seeks to promote labor, environmental, and social standards for the production of goods, especially when they are being imported into developed countries such as the United States or Europe. This has led to calls for international agreements which would enshrine these principles within global trade rules. The idea of fair trade has been around since at least 1946 with the founding of the Fair Trade Association (FTA), but its modern incarnation began in the late-20th century amidst growing concern about exploitative working conditions and poor wages in developing nations producing exports for wealthier ones. In recent years, this concept has become increasingly popular among consumers who want to ensure their purchases do not come at the expense of workers’ rights or the environment. It has even given rise to certification systems like Fairtrade International, which provides independent verification of products meeting certain ethical criteria. However, despite some successes, there remain significant challenges to making fair trade a reality, including lack of enforcement mechanisms and resistance from powerful corporations. Nonetheless, advocates continue to push for greater regulation of global markets so that all producers can benefit fairly from international commerce.

Programming for Everybody (Getting Started with Python)
output:
This course will introduce students to programming using the Python language, and cover topics such as variables, functions, loops, classes, objects, data structures, and algorithms. The goal of this class is to provide students with the skills necessary to become proficient programmers in any language they choose to use. 

Chapter 1 - Getting Started With Python: This chapter introduces the basics of the Python language including syntax, variable declarations, types, operators, control flow, functions, and modules. It also covers setting up a development environment and writing simple programs.

Chapter 2 – Variables & Types: In this chapter we discuss how variables are used to store values in computer programs, different types of variables, and how type annotations can help us write more efficient code. We also explore basic data types like integers, floats, strings, lists, tuples, sets, and dictionaries.

Chapter 3 – Loops & Functions: This chapter explains two important concepts in programming; looping constructs which allow you to execute certain blocks of code multiple times, and functions which enable you to create reusable pieces of code. We’ll look at various loop constructs such as while, for, and break statements, as well as different function design patterns.

Chapter 4 – Classes & Objects: This chapter explores one of the core features of object-oriented programming languages—classes and objects. We’ll learn about inheritance, polymorphism, and other key concepts related to creating custom objects from existing ones.

Chapter 5 – Data Structures & Algorithms: This chapter dives into some of the most common data structures and algorithms used by programmers today. We’ll examine arrays, stacks, queues, linked lists, trees, hashing, sorting, and searching techniques.

By the end of this course, students should have gained enough knowledge to confidently start coding their own applications or libraries in any language they choose.

instruction: Give students tips on how to keep their nerves under control during class presentations.
output:
1. Prepare in advance - Take time beforehand to plan out your presentation and practice it several times so you are familiar with what you need to say, as well as any visuals or props you may use. 
2. Speak slowly and clearly – Make sure to take deep breaths when needed and speak at a slow pace. This will help calm your nervous energy and make it easier for others to understand what you’re saying. 
3. Focus on eye contact – Making direct eye contact with those listening can help build trust and show them that you're confident in what you have to share. 
4. Use gestures if possible– Adding hand movements and facial expressions can help engage the audience and convey enthusiasm about your topic. 
5. Smile!- A smile conveys warmth and confidence which helps put people at ease. 
6. Don’t be afraid to pause– Taking short pauses throughout your speech allows listeners to process information and gives you time to collect yourself if necessary. 
7. Be open to questions– Allowing time for Q&A after your presentation shows that you value feedback from the group. It also provides opportunities to clarify misunderstandings or elaborate further on certain points.  
8. Stay positive– Even if things don’t go according to plan, staying positive and focused on the end goal of sharing knowledge can help maintain composure.

instruction:
Provide a detailed explanation of the steps that are required in order to construct the given project.

Steam Engine Using an Empty Beer Can
output:
1. Gather your materials - You will need an empty beer can, some screws and washers, a rubber band or string, a pencil/pen, duct tape, and a match. 
2. Prepare the can – Clean out any debris from inside the can with a vacuum cleaner. Drill two holes on either side of the lid for ventilation purposes. Make sure not to drill too deep as you don’t want to go through the bottom of the can. Use a file to smoothen the edges so they won't cause injury when handling it later.
3. Assemble the parts– Securely attach the lid onto one end of the can using duct tape. Attach the other end of the can to the base plate by inserting four screws and washers into each hole. Tighten them securely but do not over-tighten. Connect the rubber band around the top opening of the can and tie a knot at the back. This will act as a valve which allows air to enter while preventing water from escaping.
4. Filling up the steam engine – Place a pot filled with hot water on the stove until it starts boiling. Once done, use a funnel to pour the hot water slowly into the can. As the water level rises, open the valve slightly to allow more water to fill the chamber. Repeat this process until all the way full.
5. Starting the Steam Engine – Light a match and place it near the opening of the valve. The heat should ignite the air inside the can causing it to expand rapidly. This expansion force will push against the walls of the can forcing the water to move forward. After about five minutes, the steam engine should start running. Adjust the valve accordingly if needed to control its speed.

instruction:
Answer the following literature question as accurately as possible based on your knowledge of literature for high school students.

What does the green light symbolize at the end of the book?
output: The green light at the end of The Great Gatsby by F. Scott Fitzgerald can be interpreted in several ways, but it generally represents hope and opportunity. It serves to highlight the American Dream and its promise of success through hard work and determination. To Jay Gatsby, the green light across the bay stands for his unattainable dreams of rekindling love with Daisy Buchanan; he believes if he could only reach out and grasp this distant goal, all of his other desires would come true. For Nick Carraway, the green light signifies the possibility of achieving financial security and social mobility. In addition, the color green has long been associated with life, growth, and renewal—all qualities which are present throughout the novel. Ultimately, the green light serves as both a physical and metaphorical representation of hope, ambition, and potential.

instruction: Develop a mental exercise that can help people manage their anxiety and explain how it works.
output: This simple yet effective mental exercise helps to reduce stress, calm anxious thoughts, and improve overall wellbeing by focusing on breathing in a specific way. It's called 4-7-8 Breathing Exercise because you breathe in for four seconds, hold your breath for seven seconds, exhale for eight seconds. Here’s what happens when you do this exercise: When you take a deep breath in through your nose, count to four; then pause for a full second before you start counting again as you slowly exhale through your mouth (or nose) over a period of seven counts; finally, hold your breath for another eight counts before taking one last slow breath out through your nose or mouth back up to four seconds. This cycle should be repeated three times for best results. The key here is to focus entirely on your breathing – don’t think about anything else during the process! Allow yourself to become completely absorbed with each breath, feeling the air enter your body and leaving it just as gently. As you practice this technique regularly, you will find that it becomes easier to relax and let go of worries and tension. You may also notice improved concentration and clarity of mind.

instruction: Give me an idea of the mental exercises you do to inspire yourself.
output:
I have several different types of mental exercises I use to stay inspired and motivated. One exercise involves setting goals for myself, both short-term and long-term. This helps keep my focus on what I want to achieve in life and gives me something to strive towards. Another type of exercise is brainstorming; this can be done alone or with others. Brainstorming allows me to explore ideas freely without judgement and come up with creative solutions to problems. It also encourages collaboration which can lead to new perspectives and insights. 
Another important exercise is journaling. Writing down thoughts and feelings helps clarify your mind and get them out of your head so they don't overwhelm you. Journaling can help identify patterns in thinking and behavior as well as provide insight into how to move forward. Finally, meditation has been proven to reduce stress levels and increase concentration. Taking time each day to sit quietly and clear your mind can give you clarity about where you are going and why it matters.
These are just some of the many techniques I employ to remain focused and energized. By engaging in these activities regularly, I am able to maintain a positive attitude and approach to life while keeping my creativity flowing.

instruction:
Answer the following question.

How do you say "good evening" in French.
output:
In French, how to say “Good Evening” depends on whether or not it’s already dark outside when you want to greet someone. 
If it's still light out and you would like to wish them a good night, then you can use Bonsoir (pronounced bone-sher). This translates literally as 'evening'. It is used both for daytime and night time greetings. 
However if it has become fully dark by the time you are speaking with your friend/family member etc., then you should use Bonne Nuit instead (pronounced bon-nwah nwee). This means ‘good night’ and is only appropriate after sunset. 
So depending on what time of day it is, you will need to adjust your phrase accordingly!

instruction:
What sound does this make?

好
output:
The Chinese character 好 (hǎo) has several meanings, including "good", "excellent" and "very". It can be used as an adjective to describe something positive or desirable. In spoken language it often serves as an expression of agreement or approval; for example, when someone says “哪里有儿?” ("Where's there?"), you might respond with "好!" ("Good!"). The pronunciation of hǎo in Mandarin is [xǎu].

In written form, the character consists of two parts - a radical indicating its meaning and a phonetic component which indicates how the word should be pronounced. The radical here is 女 (nǚ), which means "woman," but it also stands for other words related to women such as "beautiful." The phonetic part is 臧 (zhāng). This part tells us how to pronounce the word - it is pronounced like the English letter 'a'. So together we get the word 好 (hǎo): nǚ + zhāng = hao.

instruction: Give a grammar tip on how to end a German verb.
output:
When speaking or writing in German, it's important to know when and how to use certain grammatical forms at the ends of sentences. One such form is the -te ending which indicates the past tense (e.g., gehen -> ging). This article will explain what this ending means and provide some examples so you can practice using it correctly. 
The -te ending is used for verbs in their past participle form. The past participle is formed by adding -t or -d to the base verb depending on its sound. For example, "gehen" becomes "ging". In order to make the sentence agree with the past participle, one must add -te after the verb. So, if the verb is "gehen", then the correct way to say the sentence would be "ich bin gegangen." ("Ich" is the subject pronoun.) 
Here are some more examples of verbs in their past participle form followed by the -te ending: haben (haben), laufen (gelaufen), fahren (gefahren), essen (essen), lesen (gelezen). As you can see from these examples, the -te ending is only used for verbs in their past participle form. It should not be used for other parts of speech like adverbs or prepositions.

output:
No, they do not have a lot of bread. 
In fact, when Lucie says "Nous" (which means "we") she is referring to herself and her granddaughter, Lin. She is saying that there isn't any bread at their house. When Lin responds with "Ah!" it shows surprise or agreement. In this case, she agrees with Lucie that they don't have any bread. instruction: Come up with healthy and easy dinner ideas for weeknights.
output:
1. Grilled Salmon Fillet with Asparagus & Pineapple Salsa – This meal takes less than 30 minutes to prepare, making it perfect for busy weekday nights! Start by marinating salmon fillets in olive oil, lime juice, garlic powder, and pepper then grilling them over medium-high heat until cooked through. Serve alongside asparagus spears tossed in olive oil, balsamic vinegar, and freshly squeezed lemon juice, along with pineapple chunks drizzled with honey and sprinkled with cinnamon.
2. Baked Chicken Fingers with Honey Mustard Dipping Sauce – Preheat your oven to 450°F and line two baking sheets with parchment paper. Cut boneless, skinless chicken breasts into strips and place on the prepared baking sheet. Brush each piece of chicken with melted butter and season liberally with salt and pepper. Bake for 8–10 minutes or until golden brown. Meanwhile, make a quick dipping sauce by combining honey mustard, ketchup, and mayonnaise together. Enjoy!
3. Broccoli Cheese Casserole – Combine creamy cheeses like ricotta and cheddar with crumbled bacon and chopped broccoli florets in this one dish wonder. Top with breadcrumbs and bake at 375°F for 25 minutes. Serve warm and enjoy!
4. Spicy Tuna Wraps – Make these wraps ahead of time so they’re ready when you are! Simply mix tuna, mayo, Sriracha hot sauce, scallions, and sesame seeds together and fill lettuce leaves with the mixture. Heat in the microwave for about 30 seconds before serving.
5. Eggplant Parmesan – A classic Italian favorite made even easier with eggplant instead of traditional pasta noodles. Marinate eggplant slices in a mixture of olive oil, garlic, and red wine vinegar then layer onto a greased baking sheet. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese and bake at 375°F for 20 minutes. Serve with tomato sauce and fresh basil.

instruction:

popcorn
output:
One easy way to make popcorn taste better is by adding some spices or herbs like cinnamon, nutmeg, paprika, garlic powder and onion powder. You can also add melted butter, margarine, or oil to give it more flavour. Adding dried fruit such as raisins, cranberries, apricots, or almonds will also enhance its flavours. For a savory version, you could try sprinkling cheese, parmesan, or crumbled bacon over your popcorn. 
Another trick is to use different types of seasonings in combination with each other. Try mixing salt, pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, and any other desired spice together before popping. instruction: Suggest a recipe for a vegan dessert.

Ingredients (Makes one large serving):
-1/2 cup unsweetened almond milk
-3 tablespoons coconut oil, melted
-4 oz dark chocolate, chopped
-6 raspberries
-1 teaspoon vanilla extract
-1/2 cup raw cashews
Instructions:

instruction:
Prove the given statement using your information.

"If x is a positive integer or a solution to x+3>4, then x>0 and x>12."
output:
We will prove this statement by contradiction. 
Suppose there exists some value of X for which it is not true; i.e., X = -3 or X = 15. In either case, we can see that X + 3 > 4 since both values of X are greater than zero. However, if X = -3, then (X + 3) > 4 implies that (-3 + 3) > 4, which is false. Similarly, if X = 15, then (X + 3) > 4 implies that (15 + 3) > 4, again resulting in a falsehood. Thus, our assumption of a counterexample has been disproved, meaning that the original statement must be true.

instruction:
Based on the facts that have been provided, prove the following statement.

Statement: "For every integer k, k^2 + 2k + 1 ≥ 0"
Fact: The square of any real number is non-negative.
output: We will show that for all integers k, (k^2) + 2(k) + 1 >= 0 by using mathematical induction. For the base case when n = 1, we can see that (1^2) + 2(1) + 1 = 3 + 2 + 1 > 0 which holds true since the sum of three positive numbers is always greater than zero. Now let us assume that our claim holds true for some smaller value of n and we want to show it also holds true for larger values of n as well. Let's consider the case where n = k+1. Then, (k+1)^2 + 2(k+1) + 1 = (k^2) + 2(k) + 1 + 2(k+1) + 1 = (k^2) + 4(k) + 3 > 0 because the sum of four positive numbers is still greater than zero. Thus, our claim follows from the inductive hypothesis and thus proves our original statement.

instruction:
Verify the correctness of the given statement.

"For all integers j and k, if j and k are odd, then jk is odd."
output:
The statement "If j and k are both odd numbers, then their product jk will also be odd" is true. This can be proven by mathematical induction on n = j + k. If n = 1 or 2, it's easy to see that the result holds since in those cases either j or k (or both) must be even for the product to be even. Now assume the result holds up to some larger number n > 2. Then we have two cases to consider depending on whether j and/or k are odd or even. 

Case 1: Suppose only one of j or k is odd. Without loss of generality let us say that j is odd. Since j is odd, its square root cannot be an integer so j^2 is not divisible by any integer other than itself. Therefore, when multiplied with another odd number k, jk will remain odd as well. 

Case 2: Both j and k are odd. Again without loss of generality suppose that j is odd and k is even. When j and k are multiplied together, they form a perfect square which means that their product is divisible by four. But since j is odd, this implies that jk is also odd. Thus, our assumption that the result holds up to some larger number n > 2 is validated.

If you get enough exercise to eat a large candy bar every day without gaining weight, it wouldn’t be a problem to eat an avocado every day. Other wise you should probably eat them sparingly.
output:
"Are Avocados Really as Healthy As We Think They Are?"

Avocados have become increasingly popular among health-conscious eaters due to their high fat content and supposed nutritional benefits. But are they really as beneficial as we think? In this article, I will explore some of the potential downfalls of consuming too many avocados and discuss how these can affect our overall dietary habits.  

First off, let's look at the calorie count of one average sized avocado - around two hundred grams. A single avocado contains roughly thirty five grams of total fat, which equates to about three hundred and twenty four calories! This means that if someone were to consume just one avocado per day, they would easily exceed their recommended daily intake of fats (which is typically between sixteen and seventeen percent of total caloric intake). Furthermore, since most people do not engage in regular physical activity, consuming such a high number of calories could lead to unwanted weight gain. Therefore, it is important to keep track of how much avocado we are actually consuming on any given day. 

Another issue with excessive consumption of avocados is its effect on cholesterol levels. While monounsaturated fats found in avocados may help lower LDL ("bad") cholesterol levels, they also increase HDL ("good") cholesterol levels significantly more than other types of fats like butter or margarine. Thus, while consuming moderate amounts of avocados may benefit heart health, going beyond what is considered "moderate" can cause harm by raising HDL levels too far. Again, monitoring our intake of avocados is key here. 

Finally, there is the concern of environmental impact. The production of avocados requires significant water usage and land use, both of which contribute to global warming and climate change. Additionally, the transportation of avocados contributes to air pollution and carbon emissions. So when considering whether or not to include avocados into our diets, it is important to consider all aspects of sustainability and environmental responsibility. 

In conclusion, although avocados contain numerous essential vitamins and minerals, consuming them in abundance can lead to unintended consequences. It is therefore important to monitor our intake of avocados so that we can make sure we are getting the maximum health benefits possible without compromising our own wellbeing or the environment.

instruction:
Give a brief explanation of the requested part of the paper.

Paper: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks that include an encoder and a decoder. The best performing models also connect the encoder and decoder through an attention mechanism. We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely. Experiments on two machine translation tasks show these models to be superior in quality while being more parallelizable and requiring significantly less time to train. Our model achieves 28.4 BLEU on the WMT 2014 English-to-German translation task, improving over the existing best results, including ensembles, by over 2 BLEU. On the WMT 2014 English-to-French translation task, our model establishes a new single-model state-of-the-art BLEU score of 41.8 after training for 3.5 days on eight GPUs, a small fraction of the training costs of the best models from the literature. We show that the Transformer generalizes well to other tasks by applying it successfully to English constituency parsing both with large and limited training data.
Explain: The dominant sequence transduction models
output: The dominant sequence transduction models used today rely heavily on deep learning architectures such as Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN). These models use multiple layers of neurons connected together via weights which allow them to learn representations of sequences of input tokens. They then apply this learned representation when making predictions about unseen inputs. However, these models can require significant amounts of computing power and time to train due to their complexity. To address this issue, we present a simpler alternative - the Transformer - which uses only self-attention mechanisms instead of RNNs/CNNs. This allows us to achieve better performance than current state-of-the-art models without needing to invest so much computational resources. Furthermore, since the Transformer does not involve any recurrences, it is easier to parallelize its computations, allowing us to speed up the training process even further.

instruction:
Answer the question about the paper after reading it carefully.

To evaluate if the Transformer can generalize to other tasks we performed experiments on English constituency parsing. This task presents specific challenges: the output is subject to strong structural constraints and is significantly longer than the input. Furthermore, RNN sequence-to-sequence models have not been able to attain state-of-the-art results in small-data regimes.
Question: What is English constituency parsing?
output:
English constituency parsing is a natural language processing (NLP) task which involves identifying the parts of speech within a sentence such as nouns, verbs, adjectives etc., determining their syntactic relationships with each other, and assigning them to one of three categories - subject, verb or object. The goal of this task is to accurately label all words in a given sentence according to their part of speech tagging. 
The output for this task should be a tree structure where each node represents a word and its children represent the tags assigned to it. For example, "dog" would have two nodes, "nsubj" and "pobj". In addition, there are certain rules regarding how these labels must appear in order to be considered correct. These include requiring that subjects always come before predicates, objects always follow predicates, and prepositions never take any particular case.

We present a state-of-the-art speech recognition system developed using end-to-end deep learning. Our architecture is significantly simpler than traditional speech systems, which rely on laboriously engineered processing pipelines; these traditional systems also tend to perform poorly when used in noisy environments. In contrast, our system does not need hand-designed components to model background noise, reverberation, or speaker variation, but instead directly learns a function that is robust to such effects. We do not need a phoneme dictionary, nor even the concept of a "phoneme." Key to our approach is a well-optimized RNN training system that uses multiple GPUs, as well as a set of novel data synthesis techniques that allow us to efficiently obtain a large amount of varied data for training. Our system, called Deep Speech, outperforms previously published results on the widely studied Switchboard Hub5'00, achieving 16.0% error on the full test set. Deep Speech also handles challenging noisy environments better than widely used, state-of-the-art commercial speech systems.Collapse

The system utilizes a combination of several innovative methods including a multi-GPU optimized recurrent neural network (RNN) training system and various data synthesizing techniques to achieve superior performance compared with existing approaches. Specifically, their system outperformed previous results on the popular Switchboard Hub5’00 dataset by achieving a word error rate of just 16%. Furthermore, it was found to be more effective at handling noisy environments than other commercially available speech recognition systems.

instruction:
Create a short, concise summary of the paper based on its abstract.

Few-shot learning (FSL) is one of the key future steps in machine learning and raises a lot of attention. In this paper, we focus on the FSL problem of dialogue understanding, which contains two closely related tasks: intent detection and slot filling. Dialogue understanding has been proven to benefit a lot from jointly learning the two sub-tasks. However, such joint learning becomes challenging in the few-shot scenarios: on the one hand, the sparsity of samples greatly magnifies the difficulty of modeling the connection between the two tasks; on the other hand, how to jointly learn multiple tasks in the few-shot setting is still less investigated. In response to this, we introduce FewJoint, the first FSL benchmark for joint dialogue understanding. FewJoint provides a new corpus with 59 different dialogue domains from real industrial API and a code platform to ease FSL experiment set-up, which are expected to advance the research of this field. Further, we find that insufficient performance of the few-shot setting often leads to noisy sharing between two sub-task and disturbs joint learning. To tackle this, we guide slot with explicit intent information and propose a novel trust gating mechanism that blocks low-confidence intent information to ensure high quality sharing. Besides, we introduce a Reptile-based meta-learning strategy to achieve better generalization in unseen few-shot domains. In the experiments, the proposed method brings significant improvements on two datasets and achieve new state-of-the-art performance.
output: This paper introduces FewJoint, the first few-shot learning dataset for dialogue understanding. It provides a new corpus containing 59 different dialogue domains as well as a code platform to facilitate FSL experiments. The authors also address the challenge posed by limited data availability in few-shot settings by proposing methods to improve both intent detection and slot filling. First, they use explicit intent information to guide slot filling and implement a trust gating mechanism to block low confidence intent information when sharing knowledge across tasks. Additionally, they employ a Reptile-based meta-learning approach to further enhance the generalizability of their models. Experiments show that these approaches lead to improved results compared to existing baselines.

while IFS= read -r line; do
    echo "$line"
    if [ $((++counter)) == 10 ] ; then
        break
    fi
done < f

instruction:
Given a programming language and the name of a function, write a command to show how to use the function.

Language: Python
Function: input
output:
In Python, you can get user input with the built-in "input()" function. For example, if you want to ask for a person's age, you could do this: 

age = int(input("What is your age?")) # This will prompt the user for their age and then convert it into an integer
print ("Hello", age,"years old!")   # Print out the result

You can also use the "raw_input()" function instead of "input()". The difference between these two functions is that "input()" automatically converts whatever the user inputs into a string, while "raw_input()" leaves the data as a string but does not attempt to interpret or convert it. Here is another example using raw_input(): 

age = float(raw_input("Please enter your age")) # This will prompt the user for their age and then convert it into a floating point number

instruction:
Someone gives you some information about where they want to go and what they enjoy seeing and doing. Describe a proper road trip for them and find the best stops and sctivities in the way.

I'm looking for a west coast road trip. My goal is to start near San Diego and travel to Los Angeles.
output:
A great West Coast Road Trip starts from San Diego, California! Begin by visiting Balboa Park - home of 15 major museums, beautiful gardens, and the world-famous San Diego Zoo. Then head over to Old Town State Historic Park – one of the oldest parts of town with cobblestone streets, historic buildings, and plenty of Mexican restaurants. Next up is Seaport Village – a charming waterfront shopping area filled with unique shops, eateries, and entertainment venues. Afterward, take a stroll along the Embarcadero or board a ferry at Broadway Pier for a scenic ride across San Diego Bay. In the evening, catch a show at the iconic Gaslamp Quarter before heading back to your accommodations.

Day two begins with a drive north to Solana Beach – known as “Surf City USA” due to its amazing surf spots. From there, continue on to Del Mar – a picturesque beachside village complete with horse racing track and luxurious resorts. Spend the afternoon exploring Torrey Pines State Natural Reserve – a breathtakingly gorgeous nature reserve featuring hiking trails, beaches, and wildlife viewing opportunities. End the day with dinner at The Shores Restaurant & Crab Hut – a popular seafood spot located right on the beach.

On day three, make sure to stop off at La Jolla Cove – a secluded gem offering incredible snorkeling and diving experiences. Continue driving through the hills until you reach the University of California, San Diego campus – then spend the rest of the day exploring the many attractions nearby such as Birch Aquarium, the Reuben H. Fleet Science Center, and the Museum of Man. Finish the night with dinner at George’s At The Cove – a renowned restaurant specializing in fresh seafood dishes.

The fourth day brings you closer to Los Angeles. Start out early morning with a hike in Anza Borrego Desert State Park – a vast wilderness full of sandstone formations, canyons, and valleys. Later, explore Palomar Mountain – a majestic peak surrounded by lush greenery and spectacular views. Finally, end the day with dinner at Splashes Ocean Grill – a casual oceanfront eatery serving delicious seafood dinners.

Finally, on the last day, get ready to experience all that LA has to offer! Visit Hollywood Boulevard – the epicenter of movie magic – and take a tour of Universal Studios theme park. For something more relaxed, check out Venice Beach Boardwalk – a vibrant promenade packed with street performers, vendors, and quirky characters. Last but not least, don’t miss Griffith Observatory – a famous landmark perched high above the city with unparalleled views of the skyline.

instruction: For a quick and efficient office workout, suggest a short routine.
output:
A great way to get in a quick and effective office workout is by doing bodyweight exercises such as squats, lunges, push-ups, planks, crunches, and mountain climbers. Start with 1 minute of each exercise for a total of four minutes. Do this three times per day for a total of nine minutes. You can also add some resistance bands or dumbbells if you have them available. Here's how it would look:

Squat - Stand tall with feet shoulder width apart. Lower into a squat while keeping your back straight and glutes engaged. Push through your heels to return to standing position. Repeat for one minute.
Lunge – Beginning from a standing position, take one step forward with your left leg. Bend both knees until your right thigh is parallel to the floor. Hold for one minute then switch legs.
Push Up – Place hands on the ground slightly wider than shoulders’ distance. Engage core and biceps to lift chest off the ground. Keep elbows tucked close to sides throughout the movement. Perform for one minute.
Plank – Get into a high push up position but keep your arms extended out in front of you instead of bringing them down towards the ground. Hold for one minute.
Crunch – Lie flat on your back with knees bent and feet planted firmly on the ground. Tighten abdominals and draw belly button toward spine. Slowly lower head and shoulders towards the ground before returning to start position. Perform for one minute.
Mountain Climber – Begin in a low plank position with hands directly underneath shoulders. Step right foot back so that it is behind your left foot. Your left hand should remain on the ground while your right hand moves up to meet your left elbow. Alternate between drawing right knee in towards your chest and extending left arm overhead. Perform for one minute.

Provide instructions for the given exercise.

Leg Raises
output:
Instructions: 
1. Lie flat on your back with both hands behind your head and feet together.
2. Slowly lift your legs off of the ground, keeping them straight until they are parallel to the floor.
3. Hold this position for one second before slowly lowering your legs down towards the starting point.
4. Repeat this process for at least ten repetitions or as many as you can comfortably do in thirty seconds.
5. Make sure to keep your body still throughout the entire movement and focus on squeezing your glute muscles at the top of each rep.
6. If needed, use your arms to help raise your legs upwards.
7. Breathe normally during the exercise and try not to hold your breath.

It solves problems common and unique to every team. 

No matter what type of project or goal you are working towards, there will always be challenges along the way. Whether it's dealing with difficult stakeholders, managing tight deadlines, or finding creative solutions - teams face obstacles on their journey to success. That’s why having effective problem-solving skills can make all the difference in achieving your goals. Here are some tips for developing those essential skills.  
Firstly, don’t jump straight into solving the issue – take time to identify the root cause. It may seem obvious but often we rush to find answers without taking the necessary steps to understand the full picture. Taking the time to analyze the situation thoroughly allows us to come up with more thoughtful and efficient solutions.
Secondly, brainstorming ideas with other members of the team helps generate new perspectives and insights which could lead to innovative approaches. Working together also encourages collaboration and communication between different departments, allowing everyone involved to have input and feel like they are part of the solution.
Thirdly, break down complex tasks into smaller pieces so that each step feels manageable. This makes it easier to stay focused and motivated while tackling any challenge. Additionally, breaking down large projects into smaller milestones gives you something tangible to work toward and celebrate as you progress.
Finally, remember to keep things positive by focusing on the end result rather than getting stuck on the details. Having faith in yourself and your teammates that no matter how hard it gets, you can overcome anything if you stick together. With these strategies, you can develop strong problem-solving skills that help you reach your objectives faster and smarter.

instruction:
Decide which part of speech the underlined word belongs to.

school will _keep through the winter
output:
The underlined word "will" in this sentence is a verb, meaning it has the ability to express action or state of being. It can be used as both a main verb and an auxiliary verb depending on its context. In this case, it's acting as an auxiliary verb (helping verb) indicating the future tense. 

In English grammar, verbs have different parts of speech such as nouns, adjectives, etc., but they also come in several forms including present, past, and future. The form of the verb “will” indicates whether something happens immediately, happened at some point in time before now, or will happen sometime in the future. For example, you could say “I am going to school,” implying that it’ll happen soon; however, if you said “She will go to school,” it implies that she won’t leave until she does so. This type of usage is known as the Future Simple Tense.

instruction:
Define what the underlined word means for kids.

_keep a promise
output:
Keeping a promise is when you do something you said you would do, even if it's difficult or takes time and effort. It shows people that they can trust you to follow through on your commitments. 
For example, if someone promises to take care of their pet while its owner is away, then keeping that promise involves making sure the animal has food, water, and any other needs taken care of during the owners absence. This could mean taking extra steps like calling the vet to make appointments in advance so nothing gets missed. 
It also means being reliable and dependable - doing whatever it takes to keep the promise no matter how long it takes. Keeping a promise requires dedication and perseverance but it will show others that you are someone who can be counted on.

instruction:
Expand the given sentence and give it more details and depth.

It changed my life.
output:
The moment I heard those words, everything around me seemed to slow down as if time had stopped moving forward. My heart raced with anticipation and fear of what was about to come next. Little did I know at the time how much this one decision would change not only my own life but also the lives of many others in ways I could never have imagined. 

I had been struggling for some time now trying to find direction and purpose in my life. After years of searching, I finally found something that resonated deeply within me - a passionate interest in helping people through psychotherapy. But despite all my efforts, I still felt like there were so many things standing between me and achieving my dreams. That's when I decided to take matters into my own hands and enroll myself in a master’s program in clinical mental health counseling. It wasn't easy; I worked hard every day to make up for lost time while juggling classes, internships, and other commitments. Despite the long hours and sleepless nights, nothing compared to the feeling of accomplishment I got from seeing progress in my clients or hearing them say "thank you" after our sessions. 

As I continued on my journey, I began to realize just how powerful therapy can be. Not only did it help me become a better listener and communicator, it gave me insight into human behavior which allowed me to connect deeper with people. This experience opened my eyes to the power of connection and empathy, and showed me that everyone has their own unique story worth listening to. As I gained more knowledge and experience, I became increasingly confident in my abilities and started taking on bigger challenges such as working with trauma survivors and providing crisis intervention services. 

Throughout these experiences, I learned that no matter who we are or where we come from, we all share similar struggles and desires. We want to feel connected, accepted, and understood. We need someone to believe in us even during times of darkness and despair. And most importantly, we yearn for hope and healing. With each new client, I strive to provide a safe space where they can explore their feelings without judgement and discover their inner strength. In doing so, I am able to witness miracles unfold before my very eyes. 

This journey has taught me that true transformation comes from within and requires courage, resilience, and dedication. Every step along the way has made me stronger and helped shape the person I am today. Most importantly, it reminded me why I chose this path in the first place – because I wanted to make a difference in the world by helping individuals reach their full potential.

instruction:
Explain the meaning of the given phrase in simple terms. Use an example if possible. It would be helpful if you could give an example.

"With a little give in them"
output:
The phrase "with a little give in them" means to have some flexibility or room for negotiation, especially when it comes to making decisions and reaching agreements. For instance, two people may disagree on how much money should be spent on a project but with a bit of give-and-take they can come up with a compromise that satisfies both parties. 

An example of this might be two friends who are planning a party together. One friend wants to spend $100 on decorations while the other thinks that's too expensive; however, if there is a bit of give-and-take then perhaps they can find something more affordable that still looks nice enough for their purposes. In this case, one friend might suggest spending $50 on decorations which gives the other person some wiggle room since they don’t necessarily need to spend as much as originally planned. This type of agreement allows each side to get what they want without feeling like they had no say in the matter.
